dataset: Epi Info 7 EColi sample project - FoodHistory form

pi_items.source: Epi Info 7 Visual Dashboard documentation - standard output path for dashboard canvas files
Epi Info 7 default installation location on 64-bit Windows systems; EColi sample project is included with Epi Info 7 installation
CDC epidemiological literature on E. coli O157:H7 symptoms; these are documented clinical manifestations of STEC (Shiga toxin-producing E. coli) infection including the 1993 Jack-in-the-Box outbreak

Could not directly verify field name exists in EColi project without access to running Epi Info 7 instance; verifier.py checks for 'Ill' in filter text but this may represent variable naming in task metadata rather than actual EColi project field
Could not verify without access to running Epi Info 7 instance; this appears consistent with Yes/No field values in Epi Info
Specific title appears reasonable and matches task description, but cannot verify without running application

data_details.real_world_basis: The EColi sample project appears to be based on or related to the 1993 Jack-in-the-Box E. coli O157:H7 outbreak that occurred from December 1992 through February 1993 in four US states (California, Idaho, Washington, Nevada). This outbreak resulted in 4 deaths and 732 confirmed infections and was investigated using epidemiological methods including food exposure analysis (hamburger consumption/beef products), which aligns with the FoodHistory form structure.

verification_limitations: Without access to a running Windows environment with Epi Info 7 installed and the EColi project file open, I could not verify the exact field names (Diarrhea, BloodyDiarrhea, etc.) in the FoodHistory form, the exact field name for illness status (whether it is 'Ill', 'ILL', or another name), or the specific XML structure of the .cvs7 file format. The verifier.py script attempts to validate these by parsing the XML output file, but cannot confirm these match the actual field names without execution.
privileged_info_summary: The task references Epi Info 7, a real CDC epidemiological software package. The EColi sample project (EColi.prj) is a standard sample project included with Epi Info 7 installations, typically located at 'C:\Program Files (x86)\Epi Info 7\Projects\EColi\EColi.prj' on 64-bit Windows systems. The FoodHistory form is part of this project. The six required symptoms (Diarrhea, BloodyDiarrhea, Fever, Vomiting, Nausea, AbdominalPain) are clinically accurate manifestations of E. coli O157:H7 infection as documented in CDC epidemiological literature. These symptoms align with the 1992-1993 Jack-in-the-Box E. coli outbreak that killed 4 people and infected 732 across four western US states. Epi Info 7's Visual Dashboard module is real and contains a Combined Frequency gadget with configurable sorting options. Dashboard canvases are saved in .cvs7 format (XML-based) to the Epi Info Dashboards folder.
